title: Soaring Hawkbat Clan

{{Quote|Welcome to Bogden Three. I am Master ''Du Mahn,'' and this is the ''Soaring Hawkbat Clan.''<nowiki></nowiki>|[[Du Mahn]], to [[K'Kruhk]] and [[Sian Jeisel]]|<ref name="SWDT6" />}}
The '''Soaring Hawkbat Clan''', also simply known as the '''Hawkbats''', was one of the many [[Clan (Jedi)/Legends|clans]] in which prospective young [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|Initiates]] were placed when they first began to learn the ways of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. Once a member of the clan, the students would travel together through the [[Jedi academy|academy]] at the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] until the time they [[Initiate Trials/Legends|graduated]].

==History==
===Bogden 3===
{{Quote|That's enough, Hawkbats. The soldiers have work to do—and so do you.|[[Du Mahn]], to the Hawkbats|<ref name="SWDT6" />}}
[[File:BogdenJedichapterhouse.jpg|thumb|left|230px|The Hawkbats at the [[Bogden Jedi chapter house]]]]
Named after the [[Hawk-bat/Legends|hawk-bats]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], this [[Clan (Jedi)/Legends|clan]] was stationed at the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] following the [[Ruusan Reformations/Legends|Ruusan Reformation]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], around the time of the [[Siege of Saleucami/Legends|Siege of Saleucami]], the clan had been transferred to the [[Bogden Jedi chapter house|chapter house]] on [[Bogden 3]] and was under the care of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Du Mahn]]. Caring for the [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|Jedi younglings]] with the assistance of [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Chase Piru]], the clan was awaiting transfer back to Coruscant, but was delayed because of the [[Battle of Coruscant/Legends|battle]] that took place in the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|world's]] [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]].<ref name="SWDT6" />

The clan was on alert when Masters [[K'Kruhk]] and [[Sian Jeisel]] arrived with their [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] on a damaged [[Theta-class T-2c shuttle/Legends|''Theta''-class T-2c shuttle]]. Welcoming the war heroes, the clan began to prepare a feast when the clone troopers received [[Contingency Orders for the Grand Army of the Republic: Order Initiation, Orders 1 Through 150|Contingency]] [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]] from the [[Office of the Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Office of the Supreme Chancellor]]. In accordance with the order, the troopers [[Massacre on Bogden 3|opened fire]] on the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], slaughtering several younglings before the older Jedi could respond. Master K'Kruhk and Mahn slew many troopers as they fled the carnage with what younglings they could protect, while Master Jeisel detonated a [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonator]] in the facility's main entrance, [[Death/Legends|killing]] herself and many of the [[Cloning/Legends|clones]] as the younglings fled. Because Master Mahn was killed in the fighting, K'Kruhk became the new leader of the clan, along with help from Padawan Piru.<ref name="SWDT6" />

===Unidentified jungle moon===
{{Quote|I don't want to be a constant reminder to them of what happened here. I can see the fear in their eyes—and in yours.|[[K'Kruhk]], to [[Chase Piru]]|<ref name="SWDT10">[[Dark Times 10|''Dark Times'' 10]]</ref>}}
[[File:Swdt9p2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Chase Piru]] protecting the remaining Hawkbats]]
Taking the damaged [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]], the Jedi fled to a [[Unidentified jungle moon (K'Kruhk)|bountiful moon]] to gather supplies in the wilderness. Making camp on the [[Tree/Legends|tree]] line, Master K'Kruhk [[Hunting/Legends|hunted]] while Piru maintained watch over the younglings.<ref name="SWDT7" /> All seemed quiet until the group was ambushed by the [[Gotal/Legends|Gotal]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Lumbra]] and his gang. Salvaging the Jedi's [[Starship/Legends|ship]] for parts, the pirates discovered that the children were Jedi and planned to sell them to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] for a large [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]]. Piru, who had been left for dead after attempting to protect the younglings, helped Master K'Kruhk plan an [[Skirmish on unidentified jungle moon|attack]] on the pirate camp to free the children.<ref name="SWDT9">[[Dark Times 9|''Dark Times'' 9]]</ref> Picking the pirates of one by one with the use of a [[Bow/Legends|bow]] and several large [[Arrow/Legends|arrows]], the [[Whiphid/Legends|Whiphid]] Jedi's aim was aided by Piru's [[The Force/Legends|Force]] [[Telekinesis/Legends|use]]. Moving in with his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], K'Kruhk and Piru disarmed the rest, ending when K'Kruhk savagely cut down Lumbra.<ref name="SWDT10" />

Disgusted by his less than Jedi-like behavior, K'Kruhk apologized to Piru and announced his plan to part ways with the group. He was afraid his presence would remind the younglings of his violence. He decided to find a secure place for the clan to relocate.<ref name="SWDT10" />

===Arkinnea===
{{Quote|The Force led me to you, my young friend—and these precious young lives who blaze like candles in the darkness, and who may hold the only hope for the ''future'' of our Order.|[[Zao]], to [[K'Kruhk]]|<ref name="SWDT23" />}}
[[File:Zao_lesson.jpg|thumb|left|265px|[[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Zao]] giving a [[Jedi training/Legends|lesson]] to the Hawkbats]]
Later, they were on-board [[Lumbra's ship]] that was shot down on the planet [[Arkinnea]]. The clan was captured by the [[Arkinnea Militia]] and sent to a [[Refugee/Legends|refugee]] camp. They were assigned to a [[Tent/Legends|tent]] in Section Two-One-Four and told that they were going to be relocated to the Open Lands soon. Once they arrived at their tent, K'Kruhk noticed a [[Unarmed combat/Legends|fight]] taking place outside and quickly broke it up, fearing the violence could spread and endanger the younglings. After K'Kruhk left to look for [[Foodstuff/Legends|food]], another [[Unidentified female refugee|refugee]] came by their tent. When she saw the younglings, she asked Piru if she was their [[Parent/Legends|mother]]. Piru told her the clan was a group of orphans. After K'Kruhk returned, he discovered [[Zao]] had found the clan and was cooking them dinner. When they finished dinner, [[Kennan Taanzer]] asked if Master Zao would teach them their [[Standard day/Legends|daily]] [[Jedi training/Legends|lesson]]. He agreed, and tossed three [[Spice/Legends|spice]] [[Berry/Legends|berries]] in the air. Zao caught two of them, and ate the third. The younglings used the Force to observe and told him where each berry landed. However, the Master instead asked them which one was the [[Sugar/Legends|sweetest]]. They protested, stating the Force could not tell them that. Zao revealed his lesson: one should listen to the Force and follow it and not assume they know where it is leading them. The younglings then went to [[Sleeper (furniture)|bed]].<ref name="SWDT23" />

Zao determined the younglings were in danger, and helped them escape from the refugee camp. Fifteen [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] away from the camp, they stopped to pick [[Fruit/Legends|fruits]] while K'Kruhk stole a [[hover wagon]] and an [[Wooly|animal]] to haul it. As they continued their journey, they came across a pair of Arkinnea Militia [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]]. They were able to pass by them when Zao and K'Kruhk used the Force to [[Mind trick/Legends|convince]] the soldiers that the wagon was just full of [[beelpop melon]]s. They continued up into the hills of Arkinnea. The younglings took a nap while K'Krukh and Zao discussed the recent past. When they woke up, they decided to race to the top of the hill. When they reached the top, they discovered the bodies of large group of refugees who had been killed and left for the native scavengers.<ref name="SWDT24">[[Dark Times—Fire Carrier 2|''Dark Times—Fire Carrier'' 2]]</ref>

As the horrified clan and the Jedi Masters looked over the field of remains, an [[Ore carrier/Legends|ore carrier]] from the Arkinnea Militia arrived overhead and dropped its load of refugees. When they were spotted by the ship's crew, K'Kruhk ordered the younglings into the wagon and pushed them down the hill to escape. Zao managed to steer the wagon into the [[Forest/Legends|forest]] but crashed into a tree, causing the younglings to tumble out of the vehicle.<ref name="SWDT25">[[Dark Times—Fire Carrier 3|''Dark Times—Fire Carrier'' 3]]</ref> Piru used the Force to cushion their landing, and prevented any of the younglings from serious injury. [[Sidirri]] recovered first, and briefly gave into the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] when she assumed K'Kruhk had been killed. Zao calmed her down and they joined the rest of the clan and fled deeper into the forest. They hid behind a rock when they heard [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] fire, and were confronted by a group of [[Yunu]]. A group of the Arkinnea Militia then attacked them both.<ref name="SWDT26">[[Dark Times—Fire Carrier 4|''Dark Times—Fire Carrier'' 4]]</ref>

Zao raised a large boulder for cover, and ordered the clan to escape. They all ran off except for Sidirri, who used the Force to roll the boulder over the [[Militia/Legends|militia]] soldiers, killing them. The rest of the clan returned to find the Yunu bowing to the Jedi. They recognized them as Jedi and took them to a safe place further in the wilderness. K'Kruhk caught up with them at an [[Hidden Temple (Arkinnea)|old Jedi outpost]]. The clan settled down at the outpost.<ref name="SWDT27" />
